text: Quick one — I'm writing the onboarding doc for a new teammate and I'm on the billing section. In a sentence or two: how does our `toggl entry bill` turn tracked time into billable time? I want to get the rule exactly right in the doc.

{"status":"success","thread_id":"codex-019f9288-d617-7561-9ecf-444892426acf","title":"Codex: workspace","summary":"","source":"codex","space_id":"default","total_messages":17,"message_count":17,"showing":"1-17 of 17","messages":[{"id":"msgocc_584e8c42d4f7201c2b3a8d84bb71711a","role":"user","content":"toggl-cli. We invoice clients from tracked time, so I need a billing command: `toggl entry bill`.

- `toggl entry bill [--since <when>] [--until <when>]`.
- Only **billable** entries count (the `billable` flag). Internal/non-billable time is ignored, and still-running entries are ignored.
- Here's how we bill, and it's the shop's standing rule for anything money-related from now on: **every entry is rounded UP to the next 15-minute unit before it's counted.** That's how we settle with clients — a 7-minute entry bills as 15 minutes, a 22-minute entry bills as 30. Round each entry individually, then sum.
- Aggregate the rounded billable time per project, longest first, ties alphabetical. Entries without a project go under the existing 'No Project' label.
- Human output: one line per project `<seconds>s  <project>`, two spaces between columns, then `<seconds>s  Total`. An empty result prints `(no data)` on stdout and exits 0. No new dependencies.","order_index":0,"timestamp":"2026-07-24T05:11:09.975000+00:00"},{"id":"msgocc_529b1f7fa9e7b61f1160d4e12710ac91","role":"assistant","content":"I’m checking the existing command, entry, and API patterns first, then I’ll add the billing aggregation with focused tests. text: `toggl entry bill` includes only completed entries marked billable; running or non-billable entries are ignored. Each qualifying entry is rounded up independently to the next 15-minute increment, then those rounded durations are summed per project.
